title: Modular bearing with replaceable, pivot-connected pads
abstract: A hydrodynamic bearing for supporting a shaft includes a bearing housing and one or more pad modules. The bearing housing defines an inner surface and one or more axial grooves adjoining the inner surface. Each of at least one of the one or more pad modules includes a pad body, a root portion configured to couple in one of the axial grooves of the bearing housing, and a web portion connecting the pad body and the root portion.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A hydrodynamic bearing for supporting a shaft, the hydrodynamic bearing comprising: a bearing housing defining an inner surface and one or more axial grooves adjoining the inner surface; and one or more pad modules, each of at least one of the one or more pad modules comprising: a pad body; a root portion configured to couple in one of the axial grooves of the bearing housing; and a web portion connecting the pad body and the root portion. 2. The hydrodynamic bearing of claim 1, wherein the web portion defines an I-beam shape. 3. The hydrodynamic bearing of claim 1 or claim 2, wherein at least one of the one or more pad modules is coupled to the bearing housing such that the pad body is configured to tilt on an axis parallel to the axis of the shaft when the hydrodynamic bearing is installed on the shaft. 4. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the bearing housing is configured to inhibit inward radial separation of the at least one pad module from the bearing housing. 5. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of the preceding claims, wherein at least one of the one or more axial grooves comprises a neck portion configured to inhibit inward radial separation of the at least one pad module from the bearing housing. 6. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the pad body, the web portion, and the root portion are integrally formed with one another. 7. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the bearing housing comprises an integral damper. 8. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the web portion of the at least one pad module at least partially resides in a corresponding one of the axial grooves below the inner surface of the bearing housing. 9. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of the preceding claims, wherein at least one of the axial grooves comprises a dovetail shape. 10. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the root portion of at least one of the pad modules defines two or more serrations configured to inhibit inward radial separation of the at least one pad module from the bearing housing. 11. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the root portion of at least one of the one or more pad modules is flared relative to the web portion. 12. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of the preceding claims, further comprising a fastener configured to couple the at least one pad module to the bearing housing. 13. The hydrodynamic bearing of claim 12, wherein the fastener inhibits axial movement along the axial groove. 14. The hydrodynamic bearing of claim 12, wherein the fastener is oriented at a slant relative to the pad module. 15. The hydrodynamic bearing of claim 12, wherein the fastener is oriented in radial direction. 16. The hydrodynamic bearing of claim 12, wherein the fastener inhibits axial movement of the at least one pad module relative to the bearing housing. 17. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of the preceding claims, further comprising a shim coupled between the root portion of the at least one pad module and the bearing housing. 18. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of the preceding claims, further comprising one or more lubrication devices configured to supply lubricant to at least one of the one or more pad modules. 19. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of the preceding claims, further comprising one or more directed lubrication devices configured to supply lubricant to at least one of the one or more pad modules. 20. The hydrodynamic bearing of claim 19, wherein at least one of the directed lubrication devices is integral to the bearing housing. 21. A method of making a hydrodynamic bearing, the method comprising: positioning a pad in a bearing housing; and securing the pad to the bearing housing such that a web connecting the pad to the housing at least partially resides in a receptacle in the bearing housing. 22. The method of claim 21, wherein the receptacle is an axial groove. 23. The method of claim 21 or claim 22, wherein the web is integral to the pad. 24. The method of any one of claims 21 through 23, wherein the web is integral to the housing. 25. The method of any one of claims 21 through 24, further comprising installing a fastener to secure a root portion of the pad to the bearing housing. 26. The method of any one of claims 21 through 25, further comprising placing a shim between the pad and the bearing housing. 27. A hydrodynamic bearing for supporting a shaft, the hydrodynamic bearing comprising: a bearing housing defining a first surface and one or more receptacles adjoining the first surface; and 

 one or more pad modules, each of at least one of the one or more pad modules comprising: a pad body; a root portion configured to couple in at least one of the receptacles of the bearing housing; and and an I-beam shaped web portion connecting the pad body and the root portion. 28. The hydrodynamic bearing of claim 27, wherein the at least one of the receptacles is configured to inhibit separation of the at least one pad module from the bearing housing. 29. The hydrodynamic bearing of claim 27 or claim 28, wherein: the first surface is an inner surface; and the at least one of the receptacles is configured to inhibit inward radial separation of the at least one pad module from the bearing housing. 30. The hydrodynamic bearing of claim 27, wherein the hydrodynamic bearing is a tilt pad journal bearing. 31. The hydrodynamic bearing of claim 27, wherein the hydrodynamic bearing is a tilt pad thrust bearing. 32. The hydrodynamic bearing of claim 27, further comprising one or more directed lubrication devices. 33. The hydrodynamic bearing of claim 27, further comprising one or more lubrication devices configured to supply lubricant to a trailing edge of the at least one pad module. 34. A hydrodynamic bearing for supporting a shaft, the hydrodynamic bearing comprising: a bearing housing defining one or more inner surfaces; and one or more pad modules, each of at least one of the one or more pad modules comprising: 

 a pad body; a pivot portion integrally formed with the pad body; and a root portion; wherein the pivot portion is configured such that the at least one pad body is tiltable relative to an axis of the bearing housing. 35. The hydrodynamic bearing of claim 34, wherein the root portion comprises one or more pairs of opposing axial projections. 36. The hydrodynamic bearing of claim 34 or claim 35, further comprising one or more fasteners configured to couple the root portion with the bearing housing. 37. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of claims 34 through 36, wherein the root portion comprises a base configured to receive the pivot portion. 38. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of claims 34 through 37, wherein the pivot portion comprises a rod. 39. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of claims 34 through 38, wherein the pivot portion comprises a web portion. 40. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of claims 34 through 39, wherein at least one of the one or more fasteners is installed in a radial surface of the bearing housing. 41. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of claims 34 through 40, wherein at least one of the one or more fasteners is installed in an axial surface of the bearing housing. 42. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of claims 34 through 41, wherein the bearing housing comprises an integral damper. 43. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of claims 34 through 42, wherein the root portion of at least one of the one or more pad modules is flared relative to the web portion. 44. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of claims 34 through 43, further comprising a fastener configured to couple the at least one pad module to the bearing housing. 45. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of claims 34 through 44, further comprising a shim coupled between the root portion of the at least one pad module and the bearing housing. 46. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of claims 34 through 45, further comprising one or more lubrication devices configured to supply lubricant to the at least one pad module. 47. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of claims 34 through 46, further comprising one or more directed lubrication devices configured to direct lubricant one or more surfaces of the at least one pad module. 48. The hydrodynamic bearing of claim 47, wherein at least one of the directed lubrication devices is integral to the bearing housing. 49. The hydrodynamic bearing of claim 48, further comprising one or more lubrication devices configured to supply lubricant to a trailing edge of the at least one pad module. 50. A hydrodynamic bearing for supporting a shaft, the hydrodynamic bearing comprising: a bearing housing defining one or more inner surfaces; one or more pad modules, at least one of the pad modules comprising: a pad; and a pad support configured such that the pad is tiltable relative to an axis of the bearing housing, the pad support comprising: a base; and a pivot element coupled between the pad and the base. 51 . The hydrodynamic bearing of claim 50, further comprising one or more fasteners configured to couple the base with the bearing housing. 52. The hydrodynamic bearing of claim 50 or claim 51. wherein: the base comprises: a pivot element-coupling portion; and one or more axial projections extending axially from the pivot element-coupling portion, and at least one of the one or more fasteners is configured to couple at least one of the axial projections of the at least one pad module to the bearing housing. 53. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of claims 50 through 52, wherein the base comprises an axial projection in each opposing axial direction from the pivot element-coupling portion, wherein the one or more fasteners comprises at least one fastener configured to couple each of the opposing axial projections to the base. 54. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of claims 50 through 53, further comprising a shim coupled between the base and the bearing housing. 55. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of claims 50 through 54, further comprising a shim coupled between the pad and the pivot element. 56. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of claims 50 through 55, wherein the bearing housing comprises an integral damper. 57. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of claims 50 through 56, further comprising one or more lubrication devices configured to supply lubricant to the at least one pad module. 58. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of claims 50 through 57, further comprising one or more directed lubrication devices configured to direct lubricant one or more surfaces of the at least one pad module. 59. The hydrodynamic bearing of claim 58, wherein at least one of the directed lubrication devices is integral to the bearing housing. 60. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of claims 50 through 59, further comprising one or more lubrication devices configured to supply lubricant to a trailing edge of the at least one pad module. 61. A method of maintaining a hydrodynamic bearing, the method comprising: detaching a pad module from engagement in a receptacle of a bearing housing; and installing a root portion of a replacement pad module in the receptacle of the bearing housing such that a pad body of the replacement pad module is supported on a web portion of the pad module and such that the pad body is configured to tilt with respect to the root portion of the replacement pad module. 62. The method of claim 61, wherein engagement of the root portion of the replacement pad module in the receptacle inhibits inward radial separation from the bearing housing. 63. The method of claim 61 or claim 62, wherein: the receptacle comprises an axial groove; and installing replacement pad module comprises sliding the replacement pad module through at least a portion of the receptacle such that the root portion engages in the axial groove. 64. The method of any one of claims 61 through 63, further comprising installing a fastener to secure the root portion of the replacement pad module to the bearing housing. 65. The method of any one of claims 61 through 64, further comprising placing a shim between the root portion of the replacement pad module and the bearing housing. 66. A pad module for a hydrodynamic bearing, the pad module comprising: a pad body; a root portion configured to couple in an axial groove of a bearing housing; and 

 an I-beam shaped web portion connecting the pad body and the root portion. 67. A hydrodynamic bearing, comprising: a bearing housing module comprising: a housing body; one or more pad mount portions; and one or more I-beam shaped web portions, each of at least one of the one or more I-beam shaped web portions connecting one of the pad mount portions to the housing body; and one or more pads coupled to each of at least one of the one or more pad mount portions. 68. A hydrodynamic bearing for supporting a shaft, the hydrodynamic bearing comprising: a bearing housing defining one or more inner surfaces; one or more pad modules, each of at least one of the one or more pad modules comprising: a pad body; a root portion comprising one or more axially projections; and a pivot portion connecting the root portion with the pad portion; and one or more fasteners configured to couple at least one of the one or more axially projections of the root portion to at least one of the one or more inner surfaces of the bearing housing. 69. The hydrodynamic bearing of claim 68, wherein the pivot portion is configured such that the pad body is tiltable relative to an axis of the bearing housing. 70. The hydrodynamic bearing of claim 68 or claim 69, wherein at least one of the one or more pad modules is coupled to the bearing housing such that the pad body is configured to tilt on an axis parallel to the axis of the shaft when the hydrodynamic bearing is installed on the shaft. 71. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of claims 68 through 70, wherein the root portion comprises one or more pairs of opposing axial projections. 72. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of claims 68 through 71, wherein the root portion comprises a base configured to receive the pivot portion. 73. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of claims 68 through 72, wherein the pivot portion comprise a rod. 74. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of claims 68 through 73, wherein the pivot portion comprises a web portion. 75. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of claims 68 through 74, wherein the pivot portion and the pad body are integrally formed with one another. 76. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of claims 68 through 75, wherein at least one of the one or more fasteners is installed in a radial surface of the bearing housing. 77. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of claims 68 through 76, wherein at least one of the one or more fasteners is installed in an axial surface of the bearing housing. 78. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of claims 68 through 77, wherein the bearing housing comprises an integral damper. 79. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of claims 68 through 78, wherein the root portion of at least one of the one or more pad modules is flared relative to the web portion. 80. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of claims 68 through 79, further comprising a fastener configured to couple the at least one pad module to the bearing housing. 81. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of claims 68 through 80, further comprising a shim coupled between the root portion of the at least one pad module and the bearing housing. 82. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of claims 68 through 81, further comprising one or more lubrication devices configured to supply lubricant to the at least one pad module. 83. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of claims 68 through 82, further comprising one or more directed lubrication devices configured to direct lubricant one or more surfaces of the at least one pad module. 84. The hydrodynamic bearing of claim 83, wherein at least one of the directed lubrication devices is integral to the bearing housing. 85. The hydrodynamic bearing of any one of claims 68 through 84, further comprising one or more lubrication devices configured to supply lubricant to a trailing edge of the at least one pad module.
